title: Darth Nihilus
content:

{{Quote|He is a wound in the Force, more presence than flesh, and in his wake life dies… sacrificing itself to his hunger.|Visas Marr|audio=VisasMarrOnNihilus.flac|<ref name="KotOR II game" />}}
'''Darth Nihilus''' {{Pronounced|ˈnaɪ.ə.ləs}} was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who reigned as a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] during the [[Dark Wars|era of strife]] following the [[Jedi Civil War]]. Before becoming a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], he lost everything during the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Mandalorian Wars|war]] against the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]]. He survived the activation of the [[Mass Shadow Generator/Legends|Mass Shadow Generator]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] during the [[Battle of Malachor V|war's final battle]] at the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Malachor V]], which surrounded the planet with a destructive spatial phenomenon known as a [[Mass shadow/Legends|mass shadow]]. Experiencing the shadow that obliterated almost everything on and around the planet made Nihilus crave [[Force energy]]. The affliction painfully ravaged his body while rendering him a [[Wound in the Force/Legends|wound in the Force]]. He was found by a seeker of these, the former Jedi [[Kreia]], who became the Sith [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Traya and told him that she could teach him to feed his endless hunger. He accepted her offer of [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprenticeship]] at the [[Trayus Academy]] on Malachor V, where he, over [[Time/Legends|time]], became one of three concurrent Dark Lords of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]].

Together, they formed a [[Sith Triumvirate|triumvirate]] with Darth Traya at its head and her other apprentice, [[Darth Sion]], as the third member. The trio chose individual titles for themselves, Darth Nihilus becoming the '''Lord of Hunger'''. The apprentices grew in strength throughout their [[Sith training|training]] and eventually overpowered their master. Sion defeated Traya, while Nihilus sapped her energy. The two Sith then combined power to [[Sever Force/Legends|sever her connection to the Force]] and sent her into exile. Nihilus' affliction developed to the point where the Dark Lord was forced to call upon the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] to encase his spirit within his [[Mask of Darth Nihilus|mask]] and [[Armor/Legends|armor]] to stay alive. He and Sion then began a [[First Jedi Purge|Jedi purge]], causing the virtual extinction of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Nihilus was responsible for the [[Conclave at Katarr|devastation]] of the planet [[Katarr/Legends|Katarr]] in [[3952 BBY]], [[Death/Legends|killing]] and absorbing the Force energy of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] at the Conclave on Katarr along with every other living thing on the world save one [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] woman named [[Visas Marr]]. Marr was collected from the surface and Nihilus began to train her as his [[Shadow Hand]].

[[3951 BBY|One year later]], Nihilus [[Force sense/Legends|sensed]] a growing presence in the Force and sent Marr out to eliminate it. That presence was a woman exiled from the Jedi Order, [[Meetra Surik]], who was on a quest to find the few [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] who had [[Lost Jedi|survived the Purge]] with the aide of Darth Traya, who had put away her Sith trappings and identified herself once more as Kreia. When Marr attempted to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] Surik, the exiled Jedi [[Duel on the Ebon Hawk|defeated]] her and convinced her to turn to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]] in the process. Although Nihilus returned to lurking the fringes of known [[Realspace/Legends|space]], he was eventually tricked by Kreia into initiating the [[Battle of Telos IV]] in an attempt to absorb a [[Telosian Jedi Academy|Jedi Academy]] that lacked any [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] aside from the [[Atris|headmistress]]. Nihilus met a large Republic and [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] at [[Telos IV]] that distracted him. Meanwhile, his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Ravager (Centurion-class)|''Ravager'']], was secretly boarded by a small force consisting of Surik, Marr, [[Canderous Ordo|Mandalore the Preserver]], and his [[Mandalorians (Clan Ordo)|Mandalorians]]. The trio confronted and destroyed Nihilus in the ensuing [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]] while the Mandalorians rigged explosives that destroyed the [[Starship/Legends|ship]]. 

==Biography==
===Origins===
{{Quote|The less said of that one, the better—even a stray thought may draw him.|Kreia|audio=KreiaOnNihilus.flac|<ref name="KotOR II game" />}}
[[File:Malachor.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Malachor V after the battle]]
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who would one [[Standard day/Legends|day]] be known as [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Nihilus, [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], was alive at the conclusion of the [[Mandalorian Wars]], a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-wide conflict between the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Amidst those battles, he lost everything: his family, friends, as well as his will to live.<ref name="KotOR CG" /> He was on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Malachor V]] during the [[Battle of Malachor V|final battle]] of the war<ref name="KotOR II game" /> in [[3960 BBY]],<ref name="KotOR CG" /> when [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Meetra Surik]]<ref name="Note">The character of the Jedi Exile, the protagonist of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', was later confirmed to have been known as Meetra Surik in the novel ''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]''.</ref> gave the order to activate the Republic's [[Mass Shadow Generator/Legends|Mass Shadow Generator]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]]. The generator [[Death/Legends|killed]] almost everyone on the planet's surface and in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] nearby.<ref name="KotOR II game" />

The future Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] survived the superweapon's destruction of the surface and assumed a dark persona while grieving his losses,<ref name="KotOR CG" /> in part as a means of survival.<ref name="Policy1">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'': Item description of Nihilus' mask</ref> He was trapped on the planet by the artificially-created [[Mass shadow/Legends|mass shadows]] along with the bulk of the opposing, disabled [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] that filled space around the planet—where the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] had committed all of their forces in a last attempt at defeating the Republic—and took ill suddenly due to the shadows' effects. His emotional pain then manifested as an emptiness that swept over his idle body and it soon began to manifest as an intense "hunger." Without intent, he drained the life-force of [[Unidentified Malachor V survivor|another survivor]]. The act was an unpleasant experience for him, but the hunger and his painful memories faded for a brief moment alongside his sickness. Nonetheless, the emptiness returned more relentless and severe than before. He indulged in absorbing the energy of other survivors, but, the more he fed, the shorter the hunger was appeased and the more powerful it became.<ref name="KotOR CG" />

===Sith Triumvirate===
{{Quote|There are many factions within the Sith, all seeking to take what little remains in the wake of the Jedi Civil War.|[[Visas Marr]]|audio=VisasSithFactions.flac|<ref name="KotOR II game" />}}
After the Dark Lord of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Darth Malak]] was [[Battle of Rakata Prime|killed]] at the [[Jedi Civil War]]'s conclusion in [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="KotOR CG" /><ref name="KotOR game">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref> the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] became divided and its followers [[Sith Civil War|turned on each other]], with many factions rising to stake claim to what little power could be garnered. The man was eventually discovered by the Dark Lord [[Kreia|Darth Traya]], who had [[Force sense/Legends|sensed]] him as a [[Wound in the Force/Legends|wound in the Force]]. After locating him, Traya explained that it was [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] that fueled his hunger and offered to train him to devour entire worlds to help appease the hunger at her [[Sith academy|school]] on the planet, the [[Trayus Academy]].<ref name="KotOR CG" /> He followed her there and [[Sith training|learned]] the ways of the Sith alongside [[Darth Sion|another prospective Sith]]. Traya's hungry pupil and her other apprentice each honed different aspects and skills of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], until they both became [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]]. The man took up the name Darth Nihilus, while the second apprentice became known as Darth Sion, and they joined with Traya in the creation of a [[Sith Triumvirate]]. In addition, the three each took a unique title, with Nihilus selecting "Lord of Hunger."<ref name="KotOR II game" />

Darth Traya perceived Nihilus' hunger for the Force as detrimental to the Sith's goals. Nevertheless, she taught him how to harness that hunger to make him stronger for a [[Time/Legends|time]], as she had promised. Nihilus consumed planets on several occasions, caring only to appease his hunger, allowing the dark side to consume him more and more each time he indulged his ever-intensifying hunger. Alongside his hunger, his power also grew dramatically,<ref name="KotOR CG" /> until he eventually surpassed his Master. Their views on how to destroy the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] also became divided, with Sion and Nihilus wanting to destroy them outright while Traya desired a more time-consuming approach. This strained the Triumvirate's already fragile alliance to the breaking point.<ref name="KotOR II game" /> In [[3955 BBY]],<ref name="KotOR CG" /> Nihilus and Sion combined forces to challenge Traya, confronting their teacher in the [[Trayus Core|center of her own Academy]]. Sion and Nihilus entered the Core and ambushed Traya. Nihilus [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force-pushed]] her into a feature of the platform, after which Sion larruped her into submission. Sion combined his power with Nihilus' to [[Force drain/Legends|sap Traya's Force energy]], [[Sever Force/Legends|cutting her off from the Force]] before casting her out into [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]].<ref name="KotOR II game" />

With Traya gone, the Sith were left with no defined leadership and her followers fractured into many factions once again, all seeking to claim scarce power. Meanwhile, the two Sith Lords went about [[First Jedi Purge|destroying the Jedi Order]] together, though they did so separately and with different vehicles. On the one hand, Sion killed as many [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] he could find.<ref name="KotOR CG" /> On the other, Nihilus traveled the fringes of known space with a large fleet of [[Starship/Legends|ships]] that he had ripped from the mass shadows surrounding Malachor V. He made one such vessel, the [[Ravager (Centurion-class)|''Ravager'']], his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] and led his [[Flotilla/Legends|flotilla]] to the largest deposits of Force energies he could find, whereupon he sated the hunger within him and further increased his power. Both Nihilus and Sion were successful in their pursuits and they extinguished the Jedi.<ref name="KotOR II game" /> [[3951 BBY|Four years after Traya's exile]], Nihilus and Sion, having been the ones who defeated her, reunited the fractured Sith to take control.<ref name="KotOR CG" />

Nihilus became so absorbed by the dark side of the Force and his hunger that his physical body began to erode. Knowing that he would succumb to death if he did not act soon, Nihilus ripped his spirit from his body and encased it in the [[Armor/Legends|armor]] he wore, thus allowing the dark side to consume his useless body. Using the Force, the Sith Lord was able to keep his [[Clothing/Legends|robes]], armor and [[Mask of Darth Nihilus|mask]] together, giving him some form while allowing him to use his [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]] as well as a [[Darth Nihilus' lightsaber|lightsaber]]. Apart from that, he no longer had a physical form and became simple, primitive intent.<ref name="KotOR CG" /> Additionally, the Dark Lord constructed a [[Darth Nihilus' holocron|cubic holocron]] at some point that contained a sum of Sith knowledge and a number of their secrets.<ref name="Lightspring">{{TORcite|type=class|Smuggler|The Lightspring|Tatooine}}</ref>

===Encounter with Visas Marr===
{{Quote|There is no strength in the hunger he possesses… and the will behind his power is a primal thing. And it devours him as he devours others—his mere presence kills all around him, slowly, feeding him. He is already dead, it is simply a question of how many he kills before he falls.|Kreia|audio=KreiaOnNihilus2.flac|<ref name="KotOR II game" />}}
[[File:Nihiluswalks.png|thumb|right|180px|Darth Nihilus encounters Visas Marr as he walks on the devastated surface of Katarr.]]
The Trayus Academy continued producing legions of Sith Lords, [[Sith assassin/Legends|assassins]] and [[Sith Marauder|marauders]], with Sion and Nihilus at its head. They spread death throughout the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] and turned many other [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] to their cause. What his followers experienced while simply being in Nihilus' vicinity allowed them to leech off other Force-sensitives' Force energy, increasing their own power. He also taught them how to sense their prey across the vastness of space.<ref name="KotOR II game" />

In [[3952 BBY]],<ref name="EGTTF">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Atris]] organized a [[Conclave at Katarr|large gathering of Jedi]] on the [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] colony world of [[Katarr/Legends|Katarr]], which most of the remaining Jedi were expected to attend. After organizing the event, she then leaked the location as a ploy to lure a dark presence that she had recently sensed so that she could destroy it. Atris' plan worked, drawing Nihilus toward Katarr. However, when Nihilus neared the planet, he spoke, his voice a great hunger that the Miraluka could [[Force sight|see]] and feel through the Force. The Sith Lord's hunger overwhelmed them and obliterated the surface of Katarr, wiping out the entire colony and anything else touched by the Force.<ref name="Unseen, Unheard">{{Tales|24|Unseen, Unheard}}</ref> Nihilus destroyed much of what remained of the Jedi Order, including the Jedi Masters [[Zhar Lestin]], [[Dorak]] and [[Vandar Tokare]]; Atris herself did not actually attend the conclave, though she was reported to have done so.<ref name="Policy2">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'': Conversations with the Jedi Masters</ref>

As he afterward walked on the surface of Katarr, Nihilus found only a single living being, an injured Miraluka woman named [[Visas Marr]]. He put her in a deep [[Sleep/Legends|sleep]] and brought her aboard his ship. Days later, Marr awoke, completely healed aboard the ''Ravager'', and approached the Dark Lord. When she asked him why the wave of death had spared her, the Sith Lord did not speak. Instead, he showed her a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] of the galaxy as he perceived it: beings on other planets unable to feel the Force, disconnected from one another and lost in the crush of humanity. To that chaos, the Dark Lord felt he brought order.<ref name="Policy3">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'': Conversations with Visas Marr</ref> The vision crippled her ability to see through the Force and carved out the flesh of her vestigial [[Eye/Legends|eye]] sockets.<ref name="Unseen, Unheard" /> Marr agreed to be trained by the Sith Lord as his [[Shadow Hand]]<ref name="KotOR CG" /> and developed a powerful [[Force-bond/Legends|Force bond]] with Nihilus that strengthened both; further, they could support or weaken each other in battle.<ref name="KotOR II game" />

===Onderon and his downfall===
{{Quote|You… you are the darkness in which all life dies, milord. All life… exists to feed your power, and my life… my life is yours. I beg you… please… let me die. […] Yes… this disturbance… echoes through the Force. I can follow it to its source… and bring it to you. I will leave at once, my lord.|Visas Marr, to Nihilus|audio=VisasLeavesNihilus.flac|<ref name="KotOR II game" />}}
[[File:VisasBowstoNihilus.png|thumb|left|250px|Nihilus commands Marr to seek out Meetra Surik.]]
Nihilus allied himself with [[General/Legends|General]] [[Vaklu]], the leader of a [[Onderon separatists|separatist movement]] that wanted the planet of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] to leave the Galactic Republic. He wanted to aid Vaklu in breaking away from the Republic so the planet would fall under his influence and become a staging ground for the Sith.<ref name="Policy 4">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'': Conversation with Jedi Master [[Kavar]] in the [[Iziz Royal Palace]]</ref> [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Tobin (Human)|Tobin]], Vaklu's second-in-command, became Nihilus' contact. The Sith Lord also established a base on Onderon's moon, [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]], inside the [[Tomb of Freedon Nadd|tomb]] of an ancient Dark Lord of the Sith named [[Freedon Nadd]]. The tomb contained a dark energy [[Vergence/Legends|Force nexus]] upon which Nihilus hoped to feed after his underlings performed a dark side ritual to influence the outcome of the upcoming [[Onderon Civil War]] between the separatists and the [[Onderon Royalist]]s to his favor.<ref name="KotOR II game" />

In [[Peragus II|another part]] of the galaxy, events that would culminate in Darth Nihilus' ultimate defeat took place when Atris, intent on using her as bait for Nihilus, arranged for the return of Meetra Surik to Republic space in 3951 BBY—Surik had been exiled by the Jedi years before for fighting in the Mandalorian Wars. Atris broadcast knowledge of Surik's presence through the Coreward databases, which reached Darth Sion. Nihilus' colleague then pursued her to the [[Peragus Mining Facility]], which was [[Skirmish at Peragus II|destroyed]] as a result. The [[Telosian Restoration Project]], an expensive pilot program for repairing the Mandalorian Wars' collateral damage, lost its main supply of [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]], which bolstered support for Republic secessionist movements on planets like Onderon. Surik came under the tutelage of Nihilus' former Master, who had forsaken her Sith identity of Darth Traya and retaken her original name, Kreia, following her exile. Kreia now trained and used Surik against the Sith whilst Surik herself set out on a quest to find any Jedi Masters who had survived the Mandalorian Wars so that they could take the fight to the Sith Lords who had destroyed the Order.<ref name="KotOR II game" />

Nihilus eventually felt a [[Disturbance in the Force/Legends|disturbance in the Force]] that only his apprentice could identify. As a result, Marr was sent to defeat its source, Surik, and bring the woman before Nihilus so that he could consume her. When Surik [[Duel on the Ebon Hawk|defeated]] Marr instead, the Miraluka began to question the vision her Master had shown her. She was then swayed to serve Surik against Nihilus.<ref name="KotOR II game" /> Meanwhile, Nihilus and the ''Ravager'' crew continued travelling and devouring worlds.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II||Nihilus, Darth}}</ref>

The ritual Nihilus' group were conducting on Dxun was attacked by a group sent by Surik when she involved herself with the now-ongoing Onderon Civil War, with help from [[Canderous Ordo|Mandalore the Preserver]] and [[Mandalorians (Clan Ordo)|his Mandalorian clan]] that lived in secret on Dxun. The practitioners were killed, but not before the ritual was completed. Meanwhile, Surik headed to Iziz to support Onderon's [[Queen/Legends|queen]], [[Talia (Onderon)|Talia]]. The Royalist leader's [[Iziz Royal Palace|palace]] was [[Battle of Onderon (Onderon Civil War)|invaded]] by Nihilus' Sith and the separatists. Surik turned the tide in the Royalists' favor and Nihilus' plans for Onderon were thwarted when<ref name="KotOR II game" /> General Vaklu was executed summarily and the separatists were quashed.<ref name="Policy5">{{CSWECite|III|291|Vaklu}}</ref>

====Assault on Telos IV====
{{Quote|Your path… ends here, Sith Lord.|Meetra Surik, to Darth Nihilus|<ref name="KotOR II game" />}}
[[File:NihilusDissolvesOnTheRavager.png|thumb|left|250px|Dark energy stems from Darth Nihilus' form as it fades away.]]
Afterward, Nihilus was informed by Colonel Tobin that there was a [[Telosian Jedi Academy|Jedi Academy]] on the planet of [[Telos IV]]. This was actually a ruse by Kreia to draw her former pupil there while she traveled to Malachor to prepare for Meetra Surik's final lesson. Nihilus' forces, comprised of [[Nihilus slave|servants]] who had been reduced to mindlessness by prolonged exposure to their Dark Lord and Sith warriors, made their way to the planet. The [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] was there to ambush the fleet as it approached, working with the Mandalorians who were there to assist Surik and their [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]]. Thus began the [[Battle of Telos IV]], a large conflict with fronts on both [[Citadel Station]], the planet's orbiting [[Satellite/Legends|satellite]], and in [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. Nihilus realized too late that the Academy was not actually populated by Force-sensitives, though Atris was the headmistress, but his ravaging hunger drove him to attempt to consume Telos anyway.<ref name="KotOR II game" />

Nihilus' ship was [[Space warfare/Legends|assaulted]] and boarded by the Mandalorians in a coordinated final strike against the Dark Lord. Mandalore led his [[Troop/Legends|troops]] to board the ''Ravager'', while covered by the Republic forces, to assist Surik and Visas Marr in their advance on the warship's [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]]. Four [[Proton bomb/Legends|proton bombs]] were set in strategic locations throughout the ship along their way. Colonel Tobin, his body twisted by exposure to Nihilus' dark powers by this point, attacked the raiders in an attempt to impede them. However, the Onderonian patriot relented when they pointed out that it would only be a matter of time until his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] would meet the same fate as Telos if Nihilus succeeded.<ref name="KotOR II game" />

During his confrontation with the Exile, Marr, and Mandalore, Nihilus attempted to absorb Surik's Force energy, but failed and exhausted himself because of Surik's ability to draw on the Force energy of those around her. They [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|engaged]] in a brief [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]], with the Dark Lord seemingly too strong to defeat.<ref name="KotOR II game" /> When he stunned the Exile, Marr offered her life in exchange, a show of devotion that stalled Nihilus long enough for the Exile to recover.<ref name="CSWE" /> Marr entered a trance and disrupted her link with her former Master, undermining his connection to the Force through their Force bond. This weakened Nihilus enough that the trio was able to kill him. Before leaving in the [[Ministry-class orbital shuttle|orbital shuttle]] she and her companions had used to board the ship, Marr felt compelled to remove the Dark Lord's mask, wanting to see the face of the one who had wounded her before taking the mask of her former master to the Exile. Nihilus' form, which had animated his robes and armor for years, then erupted in an aura of scarlet dark side energies and faded away as the trio left the bridge. Shortly thereafter, the bombs aboard the ''Ravager'' exploded and the rest of the Sith fleet was decimated by the Republic.<ref name="KotOR II game" /> The only relic that remained of the Lord of Hunger's former existence after the duel was his ghostly mask in the possession of the Exile.<ref name="KotOR II game" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|Lord Nihilus, you escaped death by containing your consciousness within your armor. How?!|Darth Krayt, asking the holocron of Nihilus how he managed to rip his spirit from his body and encase it in his armor and robes in the latter's lifetime.|<ref name="Legacy 5">[[Legacy (2006) 5|''Legacy'' (2006) 5]]</ref>}}
[[File:Ni_cron.jpg|thumb|right|120px|Nihilus' holocron projection]]
After Nihilus' demise, Meetra Surik [[Battle of Malachor|traveled to Malachor V]] for a final meeting with Kreia, completing her own training with the death of the former Sith. In reaching her mentor, Surik killed the last of the Sith, Darth Sion, convincing the continually suffering Sith Lord to let go of his life.<ref name="KotOR II game" /><ref name="PolicyRevan">''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]'' confirmed the light-sided ending of ''The Sith Lords'' as canonical.</ref> Nonetheless, the Sith lived on after the fall of the Sith Triumvirate. At least one Sith Lord rose and succeeded Nihilus, quietly ruling as the Republic began to thrive anew.<ref name="NEC" /> Similarly, the Jedi Order rebounded as well, with Marr and other Force-sensitive companions of the Exile rebuilding it.<ref name="KotOR II game" /> In [[3681 BBY]], the Sith and Jedi would once again clash during the [[Great Galactic War]] that pitted the Republic against a resurgent [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]].<ref name="Timeline6date">{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 1: Treaty of Coruscant|int=Timeline 1: Treaty of Coruscant}} indicated that the war began twenty-eight years before the [[Treaty of Coruscant]]. The Treaty was signed and ratified in 3653 BBY, as seen in ''[[The Old Republic: Deceived]]'' and ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', confirming that the war began in 3681 BBY.</ref> In the aftermath of that conflict, the two shared an uneasy coexistence during the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] that began in [[3653 BBY]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> The subsequent decades saw replicas of Nihilus' mask and cowl worn by galactic citizens.<ref name="TOR">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref>

{{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: The Old Republic}}
Darth Nihilus' holocron, which could allow someone to commune with a [[Gatekeeper/Legends|gatekeeper]] in the form of the late Dark Lord,<ref name="CSWE" /> was centuries-old when it came into the possession of the crime lord [[Diago Hixan]], who kept it in a cavern on the planet of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] called the [[Lightspring]] during the Cold War. A Sith, [[Vaverone Zare]], learned that Hixan had the box and she aimed to claim it for the Sith Empire. To do this, she tried to enlist the aid of a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]]<ref name="Bug">{{TORcite|type=class|Smuggler|Bugged-Out|Tatooine}}</ref> later known as the [[Voidhound]],<ref name="Voidhound">{{TORcite|type=class|Smuggler|Face-to-Face|Corellia}}</ref> without revealing its true nature.<ref name="Bug" /> However, the smuggler supported the Galactic Republic and told a Jedi Knight, [[Nariel Pridence]], that Zare was seeking the box. The Jedi set out to take the box alone<ref name="A Familiar Face">{{TORcite|type=class|Smuggler|A Familiar Face|Tatooine}}</ref> and was captured by Hixan, but the smuggler had followed her. In the ensuing confrontation, Hixan and Zare, who had arrived as well, were killed by the smuggler and the Jedi. Pridence was able to determine the origin of the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] and she set off to ensure the Empire would never see it.<ref name="Lightspring" />
{{Endgame}}

Before the [[Declaration of a New Order|formation]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Jedi Master [[Bodo Baas]] knew of three conflicting tales of Darth Nihilus' fate. One stated that he had been slain by [[Brianna]],<ref name="EGTTF" /> another companion of Surik's<ref name="Policy7">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/KOTORminispreview5|text=''Knights of the Old Republic'' Miniatures Preview 5}}</ref> who was on Telos IV' surface while Nihilus was killed.<ref name="KotOR II game" /> The other two purported that either Marr killed Nihilus or vice versa. Baas recorded these in [[Tedryn Holocron|his holocron]], from which his knowledge would be transcribed to ''[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force]]'' by Jedi Master [[Tionne Solusar]] [[40 ABY/Legends|several decades later]].<ref name="EGTTF" />

While the Empire was in power, three groups tracked Nihilus' mask to the planet of [[Volik]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], stored aboard a [[Dynamic-class freighter/Legends|''Dynamic''-class freighter]] called the ''[[Nashuaga]]''. The parties—a group of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]], Sith cultists known as the [[Apex Society]], and a band of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] called [[Sando's Boys]]—all convened in and around the [[Wavelength Gale's hidden cove|cove]] where the ship resided. After dispatching the other two groups, the spacers claimed the mask for their own.<ref name="MaskoN">{{WizCite|story=The Mask of Darth Nihilus|book=The Unknown Regions}}</ref>

The founder of the [[One Sith]] Order, [[Darth Krayt]], came upon Nihilus' holocron.<ref name="CSWE" /> In [[137 ABY]], Krayt traveled to Korriban and accessed it along with [[Holocron of Heresies|two]] [[Darth Bane's holocron|others]], belonging to [[Darth Andeddu]] and [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]], seeking their advice on how to stop [[Yuuzhan Vong]] creatures from consuming his body. The only response he received from the gatekeeper of Nihilus's holocron was a statement in the Sith Lord's bizarre [[Language/Legends|language]], which his comrades did not attempt to translate,<ref name="Legacy 5" /> but the three were in agreement. They believed that Krayt was unworthy of being a Sith as they saw his [[Rule of One]] as a dilution of the Sith ways.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|He cares nothing for the Sith or its teachings… or the Jedi. And when the Jedi are dead, he will feed on the galaxy, the Republic, and eventually, consume the Sith as well.|Kreia|audio=KreiaOnNihilus3.flac|<ref name="KotOR II game" />}}
Darth Nihilus was a man who always craved greater power. However, unlike most Sith Lords, he cared very little for the Sith as an organization.<ref name="KotOR II game" /> Nihilus saw death as the sole purpose of life, and power as the means to achieve it. He was cautious, as his actions during the campaign against the Jedi showed, and was known as an aggressive, dominant figure who would not abide a threat to his power.<ref name="KotOR CG" />

Nihilus was once a Human male<ref name="KotOR CG" /> with black [[Hair/Legends|hair]] coiled finely into strips that he tied together behind his head.<ref name="Sith Evolutions" /> After he indulged his hunger for the dark side he lost his corporeal form, preserving his existence only by having his spirit linger within his mask and armor.<ref name="KotOR CG" /> The Dark Lord fully embraced his condition, as the mysterious and monstrous facets of it instilled in people who met him a deep-rooted fear upon which he could prey.<ref name="Policy9">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/BtT2|series=Behind the Threat: The Sith|text=Part 2: The Becoming}}</ref> Ceasing to feel fear and other emotions, he became an entity of pure intent.<ref name="KotOR CG" /> Nonetheless, Nihilus was still Human enough to have some feelings, as his bond with Marr persisted until their confrontation on the ''Ravager''. She was his only apprentice, and her betrayal was met with much anger.<ref name="KotOR II game" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|The extent of his power cannot be put into words… and his perceptions have grown as well. To him…"''<br />"''You are dust motes in a storm… a grain upon the beach… and as insignificant as a body that orbits the graveyard of Malachor.|Visas Marr and Tobin, regarding Nihilus|audio=Visas-Tobin.flac|<ref name="KotOR II game" />}}
[[File:Nihilus_Falls.jpg|thumb|right|230px|Nihilus dies at the hands of Meetra Surik.]]
During combat, Darth Nihilus fought in an aggressive, one-handed style. He had learned some of the greatest of the Sith teachings, but such practices took the form of dependence. They would make him stronger, but only for a time, and he would have to feed upon Force energy to replenish his strength. For this reason, Force-sensitives and worlds rich in the Force would draw him. His reach in the Force eventually extended to a point where he could feel Force users throughout the galaxy to find planets he would then cleanse entirely, killing everything who was touched by the Force. Prolonged use of this power made him a threat to all life, as his craving grew more intense with each feeding. Eventually, Nihilus' hunger controlled him, not the other way around, and the Sith Lord would instinctively feed upon those around him, slowly killing them. Nihilus' very speech caused pain and death to all who heard it, and those who served him became utter slaves in time. His ability was somewhat similar to Traya's influence upon others: the reconvened Jedi Council in the [[Jedi Enclave]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] inferred that the Sith had somehow learned this ability to feed on the Force from her.<ref name="KotOR II game" />

Darth Nihilus was adept in many aspects of the Force. He used his dark variant of the [[Sever Force/Legends|Sever Force]] ability to betray Darth Traya alongside Darth Sion by stripping her of the Force and casting her out of the Sith Order. Nihilus could also use the Force to lift starships, as he did on Malachor V with the ''Ravager''. He tore it from the mass shadows that surrounded the planet and kept it together even though it had suffered extensive structural damage.<ref name="KotOR II game" /> Through the Force, Darth Nihilus was able to escape death by [[Transfer essence|containing his consciousness]] in his armor to continue in a new humanoid form—an ability that Darth Krayt would inquire of Nihilus' holocron millennia later.<ref name="Legacy 5" /> He was also proficient in [[Force Enrage|Dark rage]], [[Farsight/Legends|Farsight]], [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]], [[Force resistance]], [[Force scream/Legends|Force scream]], [[Force Slow|Force plague]], and [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force whirlwind]], among others, such as a dark variant of [[Force healing/Legends|Force healing]] called Dark Healing. His knowledge also extended to [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]]: during the second battle of Onderon, Nihilus' sect of Sith followers used techniques that would [[Animal bond|bend]] tame beasts to their will.<ref name="KotOR II game" /> Nihilus could increase the strength of Force powers he used greatly, if he wished, and he was capable of reaching through the Force at a range as far as a [[Star system/Legends|star system]].<ref name="KotOR CG" /><ref name="RPG Rulebook">''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]'', see "devastating power" and "distant power."</ref>

==Equipment==
{{Quote|The hunger that fills this vessel… it is power… but it consumes without end.|Tobin|audio=Tobin1.flac|<ref name="KotOR II game" />}}
{{Main|Mask of Darth Nihilus}}
{{Main|Ravager (Centurion-class)}}
[[File:Nihilus_saber.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Darth Nihilus' lightsaber]]
Darth Nihilus wielded a self-built<ref name="KotOR CG" /> [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] containing a [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]].<ref name="KotOR II game" /> It was destroyed onboard the [[Ravager (Centurion-class)|''Ravager'']] following Nihilus' confrontation with [[Meetra Surik]]. He wore a distinctive mask and set of armor, which he merged his spirit with to survive after his organic body decayed away.<ref name="KotOR CG" /> His robes and armor were black and covered his entire<ref name="KotOR II game" /> Human<ref name="KotOR CG" /> form aside from his face, which was covered by a mask. The mask was white with red stripes over each eye hole. It would have covered cheeks, but not a nose or mouth. There was also a brown embossed detail on the mask that split it vertically in two halves.<ref name="KotOR II game" />

To enable his addiction to devouring worlds, he led a flotilla from his personal starship, the ''Ravager''. He created these ships from the wreckage left behind after the Battle of Malachor V. As such, they were badly damaged and held together with Nihilus's will. He only allowed the [[Particle shield/Legends|particle shield]] to barely maintain atmosphere, and staffed them with slaves whose bodies were badly damaged from being in contact with the Dark Lord.<ref name="KotOR II game" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Nihilus' exact identity is never specified in K2 - I had a specific origin in mind, but not a name, if that makes sense, and what I say hear ''[sic]'' is not canon: He was created when Malachor V was torn apart, and he was intended as the Exile's other half, one that took a more self-destructive path rather than denying the Force during that battle that ended the Mandalorian Wars.|Chris Avellone|<ref name="Policy12">{{WebCite|url=http://www.starwarsknights.com/interview20070924.php|text=StarWarsKnights Interview w/ Chris Avellone|date=2007-09-24|archivedate=20150319151749}}</ref>}}
[[File:MiyazakiIsOnWookieepedia.png|thumb|left|200px|No-Face was the basis of Darth Nihilus' mask.]]
Darth Nihilus was created by [[Obsidian Entertainment]] as one of the main antagonists for the [[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]] and [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. The desktop icon for the PC version of the game was Nihilus' face.<ref name="KotOR II game" /> His story was later elaborated upon in a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]], "[[Unseen, Unheard]]," featured in [[Star Wars Tales 24|''Star Wars Tales'' 24]]. This was written by [[Chris Avellone]],<ref name="Unseen, Unheard" /> lead writer of ''The Sith Lords'',<ref name="KotOR II game" /> and was drawn by [[Dustin Weaver]].<ref name="Weaver">{{WebCite|url=https://myspace.com/dustinpweaver/mixes/classic-comic-book-pages-435301|text=Classic - Comic Book Pages|author=[[Dustin Weaver|Weaver, Dustin]]|archivedate=20171105192700}}</ref> Nihilus' background was further explored in the ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''<ref name="KotOR CG" /> and he received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE" /> Nihilus' name<ref name="Holocron">{{Blog|holocron|28|Darth Names and the Darth Who winner revealed.}}</ref> and the pronunciation thereof<ref name="Lightspring" /> were derived from the words ''nihilist'',<ref name="Holocron" /> meaning one who believes in "the denial of all real existence or the possibility of an objective basis for truth;"<ref name="Policy10">{{WebCite|url=http://www.dictionary.com/browse/nihilist|text=Nihilism|archivedate=20171001161753}}</ref> and ''annihilate'',<ref name="Holocron" /> meaning "to destroy completely" or "reduce to nonexistence."<ref name="Policy11">{{WebCite|url=http://www.dictionary.com/browse/annihilated|text=Anihilate|archivedate=20161228220921}}</ref> In an interview conducted by a ''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'' fan website, Avellone was asked what his intentions for Darth Nihilus were. Though he did state that his conception of Nihilus' origin story was non-[[canon]], Avellone said he thought of Nihilus as Meetra Surik's "other half," pursuing a different path after Malachor V was torn apart.<ref name="Policy12" /> From a visual design perspective, Avellone credited artist [[Brian Menze]] with creating an effective hook for the character and he cited this contribution as one of the particular things that made ''The Sith Lords'' a success.<ref name="EUCant">{{WebCite|url=http://www.eucantina.net/interviews/chris-avellone|text=Interview with Writer Chris Avellone|author=Chris Avellone|date=2008-05-08|format=Interview|archivedate=20191022184815}}</ref> Menze himself did not feel his work on Nihilus was very creative: he was asked to give him a mask based on No-Face, a character from [[Wikipedia:Hayao Miyazaki|Hayao Miyazaki's]] [[Wikipedia:Spirited Away|''Spirited Away'']] film, and it was highly derivative from that.<ref name="Deviant">{{DeviantArt|username=brenze|url=comments/1/147093401/2382796572|name=[[Brian Menze]]|text=Comment on Electronic Games Monthly cover by Brenze}}</ref> Nonetheless, Nihilus was his favorite of all the characters he drew for the game and he was proud of him.<ref name="DeviantMain">{{DeviantArt|username=brenze|url=brenze/art/Electronic-Games-Monthly-cover-147093401|name=[[Brian Menze]]|text=Electronic Games Monthly cover|quote=This was my first time ever doing a cover for a magazine. I loved the experience. The bonus is that I was asked to paint Darth Nihilus, my favorite among all the characters I concepted for Knights of the Old Republic II.}}</ref> He counted the opportunity to paint him for the cover of ''Electronic Gaming Monthly'' as one of the best jobs of his career.<ref name="Deviant2">{{DeviantArt|username=brenze|url=comments/1/147093401/1393427281|name=[[Brian Menze]]|text=Comment on Electronic Games Monthly cover by Brenze}}</ref>

[[File:Nihilus_KOTORCG.png|thumb|right|200px|Darth Nihilus]]
In-game, Nihilus never spoke a clear word; he emitted solely patterns of sounds similar to Atris' [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocrons]] found on Telos IV. [[Alien/Legends|Alien]] languages such as [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]] were subtitled, while Nihilus' words were not.<ref name="KotOR II game" /> Marr seemed to understand at least some portions of what Nihilus said to her.<ref name="KotOR II game" /><ref name="Unseen, Unheard" /> In promotional material for the game, Nihilus was seen as an opposite to Atris. Concept art showed a confrontation between the two,<ref name="AtrisNihilusPic">Desktop wallpapers released on [[LucasArts]].com to promote the game can be viewed [[:File:Atris 4.jpg|here]] and [[:File:NihilusAtris2.jpg|here]].</ref> but Atris never actually interacted with Nihilus during the game. In a bug that occasionally occurred when Nihilus died, his body was surrounded by dark side energies, but the body itself did not disappear. A Sith lightsaber, a [[Dark Jedi Master robe]], and a lightsaber crystal could then be found in his remains.<ref name="KotOR II game" /> In [[Hasbro]]'s ''Star Wars'' Evolutions action figure set, ''The Sith Legacy'', Nihilus was featured with Darth Bane and another Sith, [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]]. He had a removable hood that revealed that showed Nihilus' hair for the first time. It was styled in [[Wikipedia:Dreadlocks|dreadlocks]].<ref name="Sith Evolutions" /> In ''The Sith Lords'', Nihilus' name was never spoken aloud, but was viewable during the fight with him and in certain written materials.<ref name="KotOR II game" /> The smuggler's storyline from [[2011]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' included a section on Tatooine that dealt with Nihilus' Sith holocron, in which his name was spoken for the first time.<ref name="Lightspring" /> Nihilus' name was also used for the name of two of ''The Old Republic''{{'s}} servers, though one was called "Mask of Nihilus."<ref name="TOR" /> Following a merging of servers,<ref name="Merge">{{TORweb|url=node/316149|text=Server Consolidation}}</ref> there was no longer a "Mask of Nihilus" server and the "Darth Nihilus" server was used for the French Player-Versus-Player services.<ref name="TOR" />

===Alternate stories===
{{Quote|Her death was worthy of a Mandalorian.''"<br />"''She did not die a fool or a coward, so be silent.|Mandalore the Preserver and Meetra Surik after Visas Marr's suicide|<ref name="KotOR II game" />}}
Though Nihilus' background and motives did not change based on whether people playing ''The Sith Lords'' had Surik, their avatar, make decisions in line with either the light or dark sides of the Force, the way his story unfolded could vary slightly. A possible outcome to the final battle with Nihilus was to convince Marr to sacrifice herself, thus shattering the bond she shared with Darth Nihilus and severely wounding the Dark Lord's spirit. This rendered him no match for Surik, but this option was only available if Marr was equipped with regular clothing and was wielding a melee weapon.<ref name="KotOR II game" />

[[File:Atris_4.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Concept art of Nihilus and Atris facing off]]
Surik could choose whether or not Visas Marr looked upon her Master's face. If Surik allowed Marr, she could also have asked Marr to bring his mask to her. The mask could not be worn; however, it did give Surik bonus Force points in the game. Not asking for the mask resulted in it disappearing with his body. Dialogue options after this caused Marr to describe Nihilus' face in two alternative ways. She either responded by saying "''A man, nothing more''" or "''I saw a graveyard world, surrounded by a fleet of dead ships. I felt it through him… as I feel it through you.''"<ref name="KotOR II game" />

Had the Voidhound assisted Zare in killing Pridence and eliminating Diago Hixan at the Lightspring in ''Star Wars: The Old Republic'', Nihilus' Sith secrets would have come into the Sith Empire's possession. In exchange, Zare would have given a reward of some kind, ranging from equipment to a sexual favor.<ref name="Lightspring" />

===Continuity===
{{Quote|When the armor was transported to Korriban for burial, his spirit traveled with it, and the connection to Korriban's dark side nexus allowed the spirit of Nihilus to maintain contact with the physical world.|''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia''|<ref name="The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Nihilus_GoH.png|thumb|left|300px|Darth Nihilus in ''[[Star Wars: Galaxy of Heroes]]'']]
Sources have differed on the time at which Nihilus lost his physical body. The ''Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide'' stated that he encased his spirit in his armor after deposing Darth Traya due to his dark side usage consuming his body.<ref name="KotOR CG" /> Meanwhile, ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' stated that he lost his body when the ''Ravager'' was destroyed, preserving himself in the same way before being found and buried. This article assumes that the former is correct and that his spirit was already inside his armor when he fought Surik.

Another statement in the ''Encylopedia'' is that Nihilus' spirit was able to survive inside his armor after his defeat at the hands of Surik, not destroyed within the ''Ravager'', which was taken for [[Funeral/Legends|burial]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], the Sith homeworld. Nihilus' spirit touched the planet's dark side nexus and remained [[Force ghost/Legends|in touch with the corporeal plane]] because of it.<ref name="CSWE" /> However, two sources, the ''Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide'' and the  game itself, states and show otherwise, showing that Nihilus's robes, armor, and entire corporeal form evaporated into nothingness as dark side energies erupt from his physical form. If the player takes the Mask of Darth Nihilus for themselves, it even states in the player's inventory that it is the "last surviving piece" of the Dark Lord after his demise,<ref name="KotOR II game" /> further proving that nothing remains of Nihilus. This article therefore assumes that Nihilus had truly died in his duel with the Exile and that the ''Encyclopedia'' is incorrect.

===Cut content===
{{Quote|Our Alliance is finished. I have no need of you.|Darth Sion to Darth Nihilus in a cut scene|audio=SionMessyBreakup.flac|<ref name="KotOR II game" />}}
{{Main|Cut content from Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords}}
[[File:Nihilus_Dreads.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Darth Nihilus' hairstyle]]
To accommodate a very swift development cycle,<ref name="EUCant" /> Obsidian Entertainment was forced to drop a number of things they planned to include in ''The Sith Lords''.<ref name="Ave">{{WebCite|url=http://forums.obsidian.net/blog/1/entry-120-kotor-ii-questions|text=Kotor II Questions|author=[[Chris Avellone]]|date=2009-10-14|archivedate=20140227103512}}</ref> Darth Nihilus was affected by these cuts thus. The [[Jekk'Jekk Tarr]], a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], was originally going to be destroyed by [[Dessicus]]—the leader of the faction of the [[GenoHaradan]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] guild active on the moon—with Surik inside. Everyone believed Surik to be dead, which was not the case, and Sion approached Nihilus. He told Nihilus that their partnership was over and that he never needed him at all before attacking him. Sion was rebuffed by a surge of the Force that killed several people around him. Sion limped away from this encounter and left the ''Ravager''.<ref name="Gizka">See our article on the [[Cut content from Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords|cut content from ''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'']], derived from the defunct Team Gizka forums.</ref><ref name="Note2">{{YouTube|video=IobIhctxRXg|text=KOTOR 2 Cut Content: Sion Versus Nihilus|channel=channel/UC8qtpVxvRes8kl4g_5G2-lA|name=darthatton}}</ref>

Surik was going to have the option to claim that she wanted to become Nihilus' apprentice during her final battle with him. She could either lie or tell the truth, and if this were true Surik would have killed Marr, weakening Nihilus through their bond before turning to kill him as well. If this was false, Nihilus would have let his guard down only to have Surik attack him without killing Marr.<ref name="Gizka" />

Additionally, there was to be a section after this in which Surik meditated in an attempt to discern where Nihilus and Sion were based. What would have transpired was a section where the voices of Surik's companions would have chimed in then blended together to settle on the revelation that they had come from Malachor V, leading her there. Chris Avellone was very fond of this scene, and the dialogue was blended very well in his opinion, but there was simply not enough time to put it in.<ref name="Ave" />

When working on the ''Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide'', ''Star Wars'' author [[Abel G. Peña]] wrote a whole passage devoted to Nihilus' mask that was ultimately cut. It would have made up the last paragraph of Nihilus' section and mentioned that the mask may have been a [[Hendanyn death mask|death mask]] of [[Hendanyn]] make.<ref name="tfn">{{JCFCite|url=posts/50677352|board=Literature|thread=Obscure Droids and Ewoks comics -- translated by Abel Peña|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2013-04-27|time=01:11 AM|archivedate=20171105191827}}</ref> Those masks first appeared in ''[[The New Rebellion]]'', a [[1996]] novel written by [[Kristine Kathryn Rusch]].<ref name="TNR">''[[The New Rebellion]]''</ref>
